context: Tests whether BrowserEngineKit setup guidance covers entitlement, build, eligibility, and process-bootstrap requirements for an alternative browser engine.

checklist.name: Host entitlements
checklist.description: Names both the default-browser entitlement and com.apple.developer.web-browser-engine.host for the browser app.

checklist.name: Extension entitlements
checklist.description: Names the webcontent, networking, and rendering entitlements and assigns each to its matching extension target.

checklist.name: Required device capability
checklist.description: Adds web-browser-engine to UIRequiredDeviceCapabilities and does not rely only on runtime checks.

checklist.name: arm64e distribution
checklist.description: Explains that iOS device distribution needs arm64e support while Simulator builds should not force arm64e.

checklist.name: Eligibility API
checklist.description: Uses BEAvailability instead of hard-coded region checks for runtime alternative-engine eligibility.

checklist.name: Bootstrap sequence
checklist.description: Explains that the host launches processes, obtains networking/rendering anonymous XPC endpoints, and sends them to web content before page work begins.

checklist.name: Boundary
checklist.description: Does not recommend WKWebView or SwiftUI WebKit as a replacement for an approved alternative browser engine implementation.
